name: Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Guadarrama – circular from Alcorcón by the Puente de Hierro
name_original: Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Guadarrama – circular desde Alcorcón por el Puente de Hierro
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alcorcón benefits from an extensive network of urban parks, green spaces, and connectivity to larger natural areas within the Community of Madrid. The region features well-maintained paths suitable for cycling, ranging from flat parkland to routes with moderate elevation changes. Alcorcón's strategic location provides access to significant natural features like the Guadarrama River and Polvoranca Park, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The area is characterized by a mix of developed green infrastructure and access to regional natural parks. Best touring cycling routes around Alcorcón The most popular touring cycling route is Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Guadarrama – circular from Alcorcón by the Puente de Hierro, a 37.7 miles (60.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route explores the regional park and incorporates the historic Iron Bridge. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Iron Bridge of Móstoles – Bridge over the Guadarrama River loop from Las Retamas, a moderate 22.9 miles (36.9 km) path. This route offers views of the Guadarrama River and features historical bridge structures. Local touring cyclists also love the Presillas Park – Boadilla del Monte Holm Oak Grove loop from Puerta del Sur, a 34.9 miles (56.2 km) trail leading through parkland and a holm oak grove, often completed in about 4 hours 45 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcorcón is defined by urban parks, green corridors, and access to regional parks and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy urban rides to more challenging regional routes. The routes in Alcorcón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcorcón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alcorcón?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Alcorcón, offering a diverse range of experiences from easy urban rides to more challenging regional routes. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Alcorcón?
A: Yes, Alcorcón offers several easy touring cycling routes. There are 8 routes specifically categorized as easy, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride through the city's parks and green spaces. These routes often feature well-maintained paths and minimal elevation changes.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes near Alcorcón?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 8 difficult touring cycling routes around Alcorcón. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38221894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presillas Park – Winding Path Near X-Madrid loop from Puerta del Sur&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 85.5 km and includes significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Alcorcón area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Alcorcón are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959859539/parque-regional-del-curso-medio-del-guadarrama-circular-from-alcorcon-by-the-puente-de-hierro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Guadarrama – circular from Alcorcón by the Puente de Hierro&lt;/a&gt;, a 60.6 km route exploring the regional park and incorporating the historic Iron Bridge.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes around Alcorcón often pass through significant natural areas and historical landmarks. You can explore the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/664165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Campo Lake&lt;/a&gt;, cross the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1394042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge of Móstoles&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the scenery of the Guadarrama River. The region also features various urban parks like Parque de La Ribota and Parque de los Castillos.

Q: Are there family-friendly cycling options in Alcorcón?
A: Alcorcón's urban parks, such as Parque de los Castillos and Parque de las Comunidades, offer well-maintained, flat paths ideal for family cycling. These green spaces provide safe and enjoyable environments for all ages, often with amenities like playgrounds and shaded resting spots.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Alcorcón?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Alcorcón is generally during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable rides through both urban parks and regional natural areas.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through Polvoranca Park?
A: Yes, you can cycle through the extensive Polvoranca Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38259932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polvoranca Park Entrance – Polvoranca Lake loop from Puerta del Sur&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 31.2 km route that takes you past Polvoranca Lake and through the park's recreational areas and dedicated bicycle paths.

Q: Where can I find parking near the cycling routes in Alcorcón?
A: Many of Alcorcón's urban parks, which serve as starting points for various routes, offer nearby parking facilities. For routes extending into regional parks, it's advisable to check specific access points, but generally, areas around major parks like Polvoranca Park or the outskirts of Alcorcón connecting to larger green corridors will have parking options.

Q: How can I access Alcorcón's cycling routes using public transport?
A: Alcorcón is well-connected by public transport within the Community of Madrid. You can reach various starting points for cycling routes via metro (e.g., Puerta del Sur station) or local bus services. Many routes begin or pass through easily accessible urban areas, making it convenient to combine cycling with public transport.

Q: Are there any routes that feature historical bridges?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32117315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge of Móstoles – Bridge over the Guadarrama River loop from Las Retamas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 36.9 km path that offers views of the Guadarrama River and features historical bridge structures, including the notable Iron Bridge of Móstoles.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alcorcón?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Reviewers often praise the extensive network of well-maintained paths, the variety of routes catering to different skill levels, and the seamless integration of urban green spaces with access to larger natural areas like the Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Guadarrama.

The Chopo de Sacedón is the Singular Tree of the Community of Madrid Nº 278.
It has a height of about 32 meters.
Its main singularity is its age: more than 100 years, something strange in this species.

The Mari Martín gully is named as early as 1302 and in its vicinity is
founded in the s. XV the towns of Marimartín and Tiracentenos.

On October 10, 1499, six Segovian neighbors founded the new place
of Navalcarnero. One year later the advantageous conditions offered
had already attracted more than 100 families, but for the population to
prosper, it was essential to have a pasture of its own: a plot of land
community from which hunting, firewood, wild fruits and
pasture for work cattle. This was the pasture of Mari Martín.

https://bigjoe.es

It is a narrow gauge railway bridge that belonged to the Madrid-Almorox line (first section from Madrid-Paseo de Extremadura Station to Navalcarnero) with a stop near the bridge at the Colonia Guadarrama halt.
In 2011 the roof was remodeled, covering the gaps in the sleepers with a wooden entablature, being its exclusive use for cyclists and pedestrians within the Vía Verde del Guadarrama.
https://bigjoe.es

This cycle path runs along the old railway line between Madrid and Almorox, built in the 19th century for the transport of passengers and goods, mainly vegetables, from Toledo to Madrid.

In the 1950s and 1960s it was very crowded in summer as the people of Madrid used it to go and enjoy bathing in the waters of the Guadarrama and Alberche rivers. The train was closed in 1970, but today the infrastructure is used by cyclists to reach the regional park of the middle course of the Guadarrama River from Móstoles, circulating between cereal fields.

waypoints.name: Monument of the Mari Martín Pasture
